overrides.override_summary: Must comply with California Fast Food Act (AB 1228) — health, safety, employment standards including minimum wages. Franchisor provides no training/assistance related to Act; compliance is solely franchisee's responsibility.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees face higher labor costs and compliance burden not reflected in Item 7 estimates

overrides.override_summary: Standard Hawaii franchise investment law filing disclosure; 7-day pre-signing delivery requirement
Release required for renewal/transfer will not apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain right to bring franchise law claims even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law, but will be enforced to extent enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Insolvency-based termination is limited in Maryland

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta, GA forum selection for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may apply for claims under Maryland Franchise Law, overriding Georgia choice of law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees not bound by Georgia law for state franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: Must bring claims under Maryland Franchise Law within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Defines claims window for Maryland franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Requires 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) — overrides FA's 30-day cure period
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees get significantly longer cure period

overrides.override_summary: Requires 180 days' notice for non-renewal — matches existing FA requirement for Traditional
Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota; cannot require jury trial waiver; cannot require consent to injunctive relief, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta forum selection, jury waiver, and liquidated damages/penalty provisions for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release for renewal/sale/transfer will not apply to extent prohibited by MN Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits scope of general release for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Restrictive covenants are subject to N.D.C.C. Section 9-08-06, which limits enforceability of noncompetes
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete may be unenforceable or limited in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require ND franchisees to consent to jurisdiction of courts outside North Dakota
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta forum selection for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: FA governed by state other than North Dakota is deemed unfair
overrides.why_it_matters: Georgia choice of law may not apply in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or waiver of exemplary/punitive damages
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain right to punitive damages and cannot be penalized with liquidated damages

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require waiver of trial by jury
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides FA's irrevocable jury waiver for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: General release upon renewal is deemed unfair
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be required to sign general release for renewal

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require franchisee to consent to limitation of claims; ND statute of limitations applies
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law governs claims timeline, not FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance: removed employee no-solicitation/no-hiring provision from franchise agreement; will not enforce in existing agreements; will request WA franchisees amend to remove; no fines assessed
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees and employees protected from no-poach restrictions
